message:
🌱 kubevela: [Feature] Add support for a trait to patch to any outputs defined before it and (#787)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kubevela-1851-feature-add-support-for-a-trait-to-patch-to-any-outputs-defined-be",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kubevela: [Feature] Add support for a trait to patch to any outputs defined before it and not just the output defined by the corresponding component.",
+    "description": "[Feature] Add support for a trait to patch to any outputs defined before it and not just the output defined by the corresponding component.. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kubevela deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kubevela version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
helm list -n kubevela 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevela installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kubevela configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kubevela configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get configmap -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevela
```
Currently, traits can patch the workload of the corresponding components only. It is not possible for traits to patch workloads of other traits. This tends to make a trait overly complex when its targeting a fairly complex resource. For e.g."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [Feature] Add support for a trait to patch to any outputs…",
+        "description": "As  said, the first point, also is the main point of this issue, is already implemented in the form of `patch: context: outputs: resourcename`. However, the `context` here can override the global context in our cue files, so I'll submit a PR to fix that.
As for the second point, I think we can
```yaml
apiVersion: core.oam.dev/v1beta1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: sample-service
spec:
  components:
    - name: myapp
      type: webservice
      properties:
        image: myregistry.io/projectid/sample-service
        port: 8000
      traits:
        - type: readiness-probes
          properties:
            http:
              path: /health
              port: 8090
              interval: 20s
        # These are the traits of concern
        - type: service-mesh
          properties:
            enabled: true
        - type: mesh-routes
          properties:
            routes:
       
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get events -n kubevela --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] Add support for a trait to patch to any outputs…" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "As  said, the first point, also is the main point of this issue, is already implemented in the form of `patch: context: outputs: resourcename`. However, the `context` here can override the global context in our cue files, so I'll submit a PR to fix that.
As for the second point, I think we can discuss that in another issue, and this issue can be closed when my PR is merged.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "apiVersion: core.oam.dev/v1beta1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: sample-service
spec:
  components:
    - name: myapp
      type: webservice
      properties:
        image: myregistry.io/projectid/sample-service
        port: 8000
      traits:
        - type: readiness-probes
          properties:
            http:
              path: /health
              port: 8090
              interval: 20s
        # These are the traits of concern
        - type: service-mesh
          properties:
            enabled: true
        - type: mesh-routes
          properties:
            routes:
               - name: "api-routes"
                 match:
                   - uri:
                       prefix: "/v1/api"
        - type: loadbalancing-policy
          properties:
            policy: R",
+        "# This resource is created by the service mesh trait
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: myapp
spec:
  hosts:
  - myapp
  http:
  - name: "api-routes"                 # This route is generated from the mesh-routes trait
    match:
    - uri:
        prefix: "/v1/api"
    route:
    - destination:
        host: myapp

# The resource is defined via the service mesh trait
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: myapp
spec:
  host: myapp
  trafficPolicy:
    loadBalancer:                          #This part is generated from the loadbalancing trait
      simple: ROUND_ROBIN
    connectionPool:                        #This part is generated from the circuit breaking trait
      tcp:
        maxConnections: 100
  ",
+        "apiVersion: core.oam.dev/v1beta1
kind: TraitDefinition
metadata:
  name: patch
spec:
  schematic:
    cue:
      template: |
        patch: {spec: template: metadata: labels: app: context.name}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "kubevela",
+      "incubating",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "kubevela"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "incubating",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/kubevela/kubevela/issues/1851",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/kubevela/kubevela"
+    },
+    "reactions": 4,
+    "comments": 21,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "vela",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevela installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-12T06:27:35.891Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
